claim: A 2015 Lancet study found that cold temperatures cause more deaths globally than heat, with cold-attributable mortality concentrated in populations without adequate heating and insulation.

source.title: Mortality risk attributable to high and low ambient temperature

method: Reviewed multi-country mortality-temperature analysis, attributable fractions, and regional patterns.
limitations: Cold-spell mortality data is sparse in LMICs.
Heating-access data is not systematically collected.
